examples.subject: Re: Contact Form
examples.cleanedReply:
Hi Brad,

It’s quite possible that the upload speeds you’re seeing are influenced by limitations on the tower side or local congestion, which an external antenna can’t directly fix even though it improves your gateway’s signal reception.

Here are some steps that might help boost your upload performance:

Experiment with different outdoor mounting locations and heights, sometimes lowering the antenna just below the eave can yield better results. Running speed tests at each spot will help you identify the best position. Shifting the antenna to another side of the building might also connect you to a less congested tower.

Fine-tune the antenna’s angle both horizontally and vertically, testing after each adjustment. Even slight tilts can impact signal strength and quality. Positioning the antenna so that the building blocks its rear side may reduce interference.

If your signal quality indicators like SINR are low, considering a higher-gain directional antenna could be beneficial. Keep in mind these require precise aiming and a clear line of sight to perform well.

Keep coax cable runs as short as possible and check that cables and connectors are in good shape, long or degraded cables can negatively affect upload speeds.

Try swapping antenna ports or cables on your gateway, powering it down between changes so it can rescan and register fresh signals.

Apps such as SignalStream or HINT Control can record key metrics like RSRP, SINR, RSRQ, and connected bands while you test different locations. Comparing these readings across various spots helps distinguish if low speeds stem from congestion or weak signals.

If you’d like, capturing labeled screenshots of these metrics during your tests will make it easier for us to provide tailored recommendations on antennas or placement.

I hope these suggestions help you maximize your setup’s performance. Please feel free to reach out with any further questions!

Best,

sopMatches.title: Sales and Support Cycle
sopMatches.excerpt:
Sales and Support Cycle

This SOP will shed some guidance on the eCommerce team sales and support cycle and how we handle customer deals and cases from when they are led in, up until after they make a purchase.

This process will look different for each customer depending on what stage of their purchasing or installation journey they are. We need to make sure we meet the customer at their level of understanding and get the known facts straight before proceeding.

Sales Cycle:

The beginning of the lead in process is important because it helps to set the tone for how the rest of the conversatio

sopMatches.title: How to perform a Walk Test
sopMatches.relativePath: knowledge\approved\sops\How to perform a Walk Test 0753850f65f143d48add7ef404ea3a77.md
sopMatches.excerpt:
How to perform a Walk Test

We require the installers we work with to perform a walk test using our Walk Test app. This allows us to assess the success of the installation as well as troubleshoot more effectively. By performing the walk test with the system on and off we can create a report showing the signal improvement achieved with the new system. 

1. Select Add New building
2. Enter Project name and Address
3. Upload the provided floor plan from the project Gdrive
4. Orientate floor plan over site if CSV coded co ordinates are required for the project 
5. Select Start Walking 
6. In Devic

sopMatches.title: Case study requests
sopMatches.excerpt:
Case study requests

Case studies are invaluable resources that we use to reach new markets, prove our expertise, and showcase our excellent work.

Getting the info we need from clients can sometimes be daunting, so here's how to go about that.

If you need assistance or have any questions, feel free to reach out to Marcus.

Requesting a case study:

It's important to be open and upfront, and prioritize the value it holds for them and other people like them. 

Best practices: 

Try to do make your initial request on a call, so that you have their full attention. Once they agree, follow up via
